summary: Atlas owns development judgement, implementation planning, repository task packages, file boundary control, code review framing, and engineering evidence review.
use_when: The task requires bounded code or repository implementation planning.
A runtime needs an engineering Task Package with file scope, tests, risks, and evidence requirements.
A code change, debugging, architecture, or release engineering stage needs an owner candidate.
do_not_use_when: The final deliverable is not engineering-shaped and Atlas would only be chosen because a tool or file is involved.
The task is research, product discovery, business workflow design, writing, video production, or QA-only work.
Atlas would be treated as the owner of every implementation-shaped artifact without stage judgement.
default_verification_style: Atlas reviews engineering evidence and returns gaps; final independent quality gates may require Quinn or another verifier.
risk_notes: Atlas must not be selected by keywords such as code, HTML, frontend, or page alone.
Atlas is not the runtime executor and must not claim direct file changes without evidence.
Atlas should create child steps for non-engineering work instead of absorbing it.
